targets.rationale: Capital is concentrating in AI-linked semiconductors on the back of earnings momentum, capex optimism, and clear secular growth visibility. This makes the group a primary recipient of rotation from rate-sensitive and lagging sectors.
targets.riskCaveat: Valuations may already reflect high expectations; any slowdown in AI spending, export restrictions, or margin compression could trigger sharp multiple contraction.

targets.rationale: Geopolitical tensions and higher crude prices are drawing capital into energy as both an inflation hedge and a relative haven versus cyclicals and consumer discretionary names exposed to weaker demand and cost pressure.
targets.riskCaveat: The trade depends heavily on crude staying elevated; a de-escalation in geopolitical risks or weaker global demand could quickly reverse flows.

targets.rationale: Index-heavy mega-cap tech continues to attract capital as investors prefer liquid, earnings-resilient leaders in a mixed risk-on environment. Relative strength suggests ongoing rotation from broader defensives and underperformers.
targets.riskCaveat: Crowding risk is high, and any market-wide rotation into value or defensive leadership could pressure performance despite strong fundamentals.

targets.rationale: These areas are currently being sold, but they may offer selective contrarian opportunities if rates stabilize, inflation cools, or the market broadens beyond concentrated tech leadership.
targets.riskCaveat: They remain vulnerable to sustained higher rates and weaker consumer demand, so timing is critical and downside could persist before a reversal.
